title: Ouratea caracasana
taxonomy: Ouratea caracasana is classified within the family Ochnaceae. This classification places Ouratea caracasana as a member of the order Malpighiales. The family Ochnaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the chrysophyll family. Ouratea caracasana is a species of flowering plant native to South America. It is part of the genus Ouratea, which comprises various species of flowering plants.
uses: Ouratea caracasana has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential therapeutic properties. intro: Ouratea caracasana, also known as "guácimo" or "ouratea", is a plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea caracasana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as to promote wound healing.
